criteria.description: A net-new module under src/dashboard/ exports a pure, import-safe function that, given an explicit run-folder path (and a caller-supplied 'now'), reads state.json and trace.jsonl from that folder and returns a plain JS object of display-mapped fields. Importing the module and calling the assembler starts no server, binds no port, and performs no filesystem writes.
criteria.verifyBy: Vitest imports the module and calls the assembler on the complete-run fixture; test asserts a plain object is returned. Grep the module source to confirm it imports only Node stdlib modules (e.g. node:fs / node:path, and benign helpers like node:url / node:process are permitted) — no http/server modules and no harness entrypoint that would drag the Anthropic SDK into the import closure.

criteria.description: The assembler returns an object with these exact, stable keys: runId, goal, currentSprint (number index), currentSprintTitle (string), contractVersion (number, the round), phase (string|null, current step), status, haltReason, contractFreezeReason, budgetSpentUsd, startedAt, elapsedMs (number|null), scores (per-sprint scores, shape pinned in c3), and degraded (boolean). On the complete-run fixture these map to: runId=state.runId, goal=state.goal, currentSprint=state.currentSprint, currentSprintTitle=state.sprints[state.currentSprint].title, contractVersion=state.contractVersion, phase=phase of the LAST trace.jsonl line (regardless of that line's phase type), status=state.status, haltReason=state.haltReason, contractFreezeReason=state.contractFreezeReason, budgetSpentUsd=state.budgetSpentUsd, startedAt=state.startedAt, and degraded=false. The complete fixture's LAST trace line is a non-EVALUATE phase (e.g. DECIDE) so 'phase = last line, any phase' is genuinely proven and not coincidentally satisfied by an EVALUATE last line.
criteria.verifyBy: Vitest asserts each enumerated key is present and deep-equals the corresponding value from the fixture's state.json / last trace line, including expect(result.runId).toBe(fixtureState.runId); expect(result.startedAt).toBe(fixtureState.startedAt); expect(result.goal).toBe(fixtureState.goal); expect(result.currentSprint).toBe(fixtureState.currentSprint); expect(result.currentSprintTitle).toBe(fixtureState.sprints[fixtureState.currentSprint].title); expect(result.phase).toBe(lastTraceLine.phase); test additionally asserts lastTraceLine.phase !== 'EVALUATE' to confirm the last-line-wins rule; expect(result.degraded).toBe(false).

criteria.description: result.scores is an array of { sprint: number, score: number } objects derived from EVALUATE trace events in trace-file order (one entry per EVALUATE line, carrying that line's own sprint and score fields). When trace.jsonl contains no EVALUATE events (or is absent), result.scores falls back to state.scores[] preserving its real semantics as a flat append-log: each numeric entry maps to { sprint: null, score: state.scores[i] } (index i is NOT treated as a sprint number, since state.scores is a flat run-wide log).
criteria.verifyBy: Vitest on the complete fixture (which has EVALUATE lines) asserts result.scores deep-equals the ordered [{sprint, score}, …] built by reading each EVALUATE line's sprint and score. A dedicated fixture/case with a populated state.scores[] and ZERO EVALUATE lines asserts result.scores deep-equals state.scores.map((score) => ({ sprint: null, score })), confirming the fallback branch is exercised and no false per-sprint index is fabricated.

criteria.description: result.elapsedMs is computed as caller-supplied 'now' minus Date.parse(state.startedAt) (not from Date.now() internally), yielding a deterministic duration in milliseconds when startedAt is present and parseable. When state.startedAt is absent or unparseable (Date.parse yields NaN), result.elapsedMs is null (never NaN).
criteria.verifyBy: Vitest passes a fixed 'now' on the complete fixture and asserts result.elapsedMs === now − Date.parse(fixtureState.startedAt); a second call with a different 'now' yields the correspondingly different value. On the missing-fields/corrupt cases (absent or unparseable startedAt) asserts expect(result.elapsedMs).toBeNull().

criteria.description: An exported most-recently-modified auto-discovery helper, given a runs/ directory path, returns the newest (highest mtime) run folder path when no explicit run path is supplied; given an empty or nonexistent runs/ directory it returns null (does not throw).
criteria.verifyBy: Vitest builds a temp runs/ dir with two run folders of differing mtimes and asserts the discovery function returns the most-recently-modified one; a second case on an empty temp dir and a nonexistent path each asserts it returns null and does not throw.

criteria.description: Graceful degradation without throwing across the degradation fixtures/cases, always returning a non-null plain object: (a) missing-fields — the named optional fields budgetSpentUsd and runDir, when absent from a PARSEABLE state.json, surface as null in the returned object, and degraded === false (absent optional fields do NOT flip the degraded discriminator); (b) mid-run partial (status 'running', fewer scores than sprints) — assembles the present fields with status preserved, and degraded === false (a parseable-but-incomplete state is not corrupt); (c) corrupt/half-written state.json that does not parse — the assembler catches the parse failure and returns a non-null plain object with degraded === true (the explicit 'updating' / last-known-good discriminator) rather than throwing; degraded is reserved SOLELY for this unparseable case, not for merely-absent optional fields; (d) empty or absent trace.jsonl — does not throw and yields phase === null.
criteria.verifyBy: Vitest wraps each case in expect(() => assemble(...)).not.toThrow(). Missing-fields: expect(result.budgetSpentUsd).toBeNull(); expect(result.runDir).toBeNull(); expect(result.degraded).toBe(false). Mid-run partial: expect(result.status).toBe('running'); expect(result.degraded).toBe(false); present fields mapped. Corrupt: expect(result).not.toBeNull(); expect(typeof result).toBe('object'); expect(result.degraded).toBe(true). Empty/absent trace: expect(result.phase).toBeNull() and no throw.

criteria.description: Checked-in fixture run directories exist in the test tree covering: (a) complete finished run WITH EVALUATE trace lines whose LAST trace line is a non-EVALUATE phase (e.g. DECIDE); (b) mid-run partial (status running, fewer scores than sprints); (c) missing-fields (budgetSpentUsd/runDir absent, state.json still parseable); (d) corrupt/half-written state.json that does not parse; (e) empty/absent trace.jsonl coverage (via a fixture or an in-test temp dir); and (f) a fixture (or in-test temp dir) with a populated state.scores[] and ZERO EVALUATE lines so c3's fallback branch is exercised. Each contains the state.json (and trace.jsonl where applicable) the tests read.
criteria.verifyBy: Fixture directories/files are present on disk under the test tree and are the inputs the c2–c6 tests load; test run confirms they are readable/parsed as intended, including the non-EVALUATE last-line in (a) and the zero-EVALUATE-with-state.scores case in (f).

criteria.description: The whole vitest suite passes offline with the new tests added, and no new runtime dependency is introduced (package.json dependencies unchanged; module uses Node stdlib only).
criteria.verifyBy: `npm test` passes with the baseline suite plus the new tests green; diff of package.json shows no additions to dependencies.

scope.description: Confine changes to a net-new src/dashboard/ module, its test file(s) under tests/, and checked-in fixture run directories in the test tree. Do not refactor unrelated harness modules.
Pure reader only: never write, create, or modify any file in a run folder, and never read or write config. No HTTP server, no page rendering in this sprint (deferred to a later sprint).
Do not modify the harness's progress emission (trace writer, orchestrator, state schema). Read only the existing on-disk format.
No new dependencies: Node built-in modules only (fs/path); nothing added to package.json dependencies. Follow repo conventions — tests in tests/, ES-module .js import specifiers.

criteria.description: A net-new dashboard server module exists (e.g. src/dashboard/server.ts) that exports an explicit start() function accepting a target run-folder path and an ephemeral port (0 allowed), returning a handle from which the actually-bound port and a close/stop mechanism are reachable. Importing the module has zero side effects: it neither binds a port nor starts listening; the server begins listening only when start() is called.
criteria.verifyBy: A vitest test imports the module and asserts no port is bound / no server is listening purely from import (no listening handle before start()). A separate test calls start(port:0, fixtureDir), reads the assigned ephemeral port from the returned handle, confirms the server is listening, then closes it cleanly.

criteria.description: With the server started against a fixture run folder, GET / returns HTTP 200 with Content-Type text/html and an HTML body (contains <html or <!DOCTYPE and a <body).
criteria.verifyBy: Fetch-level vitest test: start on ephemeral port, `await fetch(base + '/')`, assert res.status===200, content-type includes text/html, and body text contains HTML document markers.

criteria.description: With the server started against a fixture run folder, GET /data returns HTTP 200 with Content-Type application/json and a body that parses as JSON carrying the mapped run fields assembled from state.json + trace.jsonl: at minimum goal, current sprint (index and title), contractVersion (round), current step/phase (from last trace.jsonl line), per-sprint scores, an elapsed wall-clock value computed against the server's current time from startedAt, status, and budgetSpentUsd when present.
criteria.verifyBy: Fetch-level vitest test against a complete finished-run fixture: fetch '/data', assert status 200 and content-type application/json, JSON.parse the body, and assert presence/correctness of the mapped fields (goal text, sprints[currentSprint].title, contractVersion, phase equal to the last trace line's phase, scores, budgetSpentUsd). For elapsed, assert the elapsed field is a finite number >= 0 (the complete fixture's startedAt is in the past) — do NOT assert an exact millisecond value, so wall-clock timing cannot flake.

criteria.description: GET /data responds with HTTP 200 and a JSON body without throwing (never 500) against each of the four fixture cases: (a) complete finished run, (b) mid-run partial (valid but fewer scores than sprints, status running), (c) missing optional fields (e.g. budgetSpentUsd/runDir absent), and (d) corrupt/half-written state.json that does not parse as JSON. In the corrupt/partial case it serves last-known-good or an explicit updating/degraded signal rather than 500.
criteria.verifyBy: A vitest test parametrized over four checked-in fixture run directories: for each, start the server, fetch '/data', assert status===200 and that the body JSON.parses; assert the corrupt-fixture response carries a degraded/updating indicator (or last-known-good) rather than an error status.

criteria.description: Request handling is deterministic and crash-free: an unmapped route (e.g. GET /nonexistent) yields a deterministic non-2xx status (e.g. 404) and the server remains responsive to a subsequent GET / which still returns 200. The exact 404 code is advisory; the graded guarantee is non-2xx-for-unmapped plus continued responsiveness.
criteria.verifyBy: Fetch-level vitest test: request an unmapped path and assert a non-2xx status, then assert a subsequent GET / still returns 200 (server still responsive).

criteria.description: The dashboard server module's RUNTIME import closure is limited to Node stdlib plus the sprint-1 dashboard reader/assembler and never reaches the Anthropic SDK (@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk), the orchestrator, the agents, or the CLI entrypoints. Type-only imports (`import type … from '../state/types.js'`, '../trace/types.js', etc.) from sibling type modules are explicitly permitted since they are erased at compile time and contribute nothing to the runtime closure. No new runtime dependency is added to package.json.
criteria.verifyBy: A vitest test imports the server module (or loads it in an isolated child process) and asserts the loaded/required-module set does not include @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk / orchestrator / agents / CLI modules, and that import binds no port and starts no server. A static check confirms package.json dependencies are unchanged (no new entries). Type-only relative imports from sibling type modules are NOT treated as violations. Reviewer/critic confirms the runtime import graph does not reach the SDK/orchestrator/agents.

criteria.description: The full vitest suite passes offline with the new hermetic fetch-level tests added, and the previously-green baseline (existing tests) remains passing; no new npm dependency is introduced.
criteria.verifyBy: Run `npm test`: it passes with the new dashboard tests included and no network access; git diff of package.json shows no added dependencies.

scope.description: Net-new dashboard module (e.g. src/dashboard/**) plus its test file(s) and fixture run directories under the test tree only. Do not refactor unrelated harness modules (orchestrator, agents, state store, trace writer, CLI).

Strictly read-only over the run folder: the server must never write, create, or modify any file in the run folder, and must never read or write any config. No launch/stop controls of runs, no config editing (stage 2, deferred).
Do not modify the harness's progress-event emission or on-disk formats (trace writer, orchestrator, state schema). Stage 1 is a pure reader over the existing on-disk state.json/trace.jsonl format.
Node built-in modules only for the server; no new entries in package.json dependencies. Tests use the existing vitest devDependency.

True push streaming (SSE/WebSocket) is out of scope. This sprint's graded surface is the server endpoints (GET /, GET /data) and import-safety; the client-side polling-loop assertions belong to a later sprint. The page HTML content of / is delivered here.

criteria.description: A net-new dashboard module under src/ exports a function that starts the HTTP server on a caller-supplied port/host against an explicit run-folder path and returns the server + its bound address, using only Node stdlib (http/fs/path). The module is import-safe: importing it starts no server and binds no port.
criteria.verifyBy: A vitest test imports the module and asserts that after import no port is listening / no server object was created (no side effect at import); a separate test calls the start function with port 0 against a fixture dir and confirms it returns a listening server on an ephemeral port, then closes it.

criteria.description: GET / returns 200 with Content-Type text/html and an HTML page whose rendered markup contains the fixture's goal text, the current sprint number and sprints[currentSprint].title, the current round (contractVersion), the current step/phase (last trace.jsonl line's phase), the specific per-sprint score value(s) from the fixture, the spend (budgetSpentUsd) when present, and a stable identifiable elapsed element anchored by a known id or data-attribute (e.g. id="elapsed") that the client poller fills — not a substring of the volatile elapsed value. The complete-run fixture driving this test contains >=1 EVALUATE trace event carrying {sprint, score} so the per-sprint score(s) come from the primary EVALUATE path.
criteria.verifyBy: A fetch-level vitest test starts the server on an ephemeral port against the complete-finished-run fixture, does GET /, asserts status 200 and text/html, and asserts the response body substring-contains the goal text, the sprint title string, the contractVersion value, the phase string of the last trace line, the exact score value(s) drawn from that fixture's EVALUATE events, the exact budgetSpentUsd value from the fixture, and contains an element bearing the known elapsed id/data-attribute the poller targets.

criteria.description: GET /data returns 200 with Content-Type application/json and a JSON body assembled from state.json + trace.jsonl carrying the mapped fields: goal, currentSprint (+ resolved sprint title), contractVersion, current step/phase from the last trace line, per-sprint scores derived from EVALUATE events (fallback state.scores[] when trace.jsonl is absent/empty), a numeric elapsedMs derived from startedAt, budgetSpentUsd, and status/haltReason/contractFreezeReason. When trace.jsonl is absent or empty, phase falls back to a defined value (null or a named placeholder) and scores fall back to state.scores[].
criteria.verifyBy: A fetch-level vitest test does GET /data against the complete-run fixture (which contains >=1 EVALUATE event with {sprint, score}), parses the JSON, and asserts: goal equals the fixture goal, currentSprint and resolved sprint title are correct, contractVersion matches, phase equals the last trace line's phase, scores array equals the values from the fixture's EVALUATE events (primary path, not fallback), budgetSpentUsd matches, and a numeric elapsedMs field is present with value >= 0 and equal to (Date.now() - Date.parse(startedAt)) within a tolerance of a few seconds. A separate test against a fixture with no/empty trace.jsonl asserts phase is the defined fallback and scores equal state.scores[].

criteria.description: The served page updates in place via client-side polling: the HTML embeds JavaScript that uses setInterval and fetch targeting the /data endpoint and mutates the DOM via a DOM API, and the page contains NO <meta http-equiv="refresh"> tag and no full-page navigation mechanism.
criteria.verifyBy: A vitest test fetches GET / and asserts the body contains evidence of a setInterval(...) call, a fetch call whose argument references the '/data' path specifically, and a DOM-mutation API reference (one of getElementById/querySelector/textContent/innerHTML). It asserts the body does NOT match a meta http-equiv=refresh tag (case-insensitive regex).

criteria.description: Target selection: the start function accepts an explicit run-folder argument, and when omitted auto-discovers the most-recently-modified run folder under runs/. A pure/exported helper performs the most-recently-modified discovery so it is unit-testable without a live run.
criteria.verifyBy: A vitest unit test creates a temp runs/ dir with multiple subfolders having distinct mtimes and asserts the discovery helper returns the newest; fetch-level tests always pass an explicit fixture dir.

criteria.description: Graceful degradation with a defined placeholder convention: the module uses one concrete placeholder token (e.g. "—") for absent fields in the rendered page. Fixtures for (a) missing optional fields (e.g. budgetSpentUsd/runDir absent), (b) a mid-run partial (status running with fewer scores than sprints), and (c) a corrupt/half-written state.json that does not parse as JSON are checked into the test tree as real directories, alongside the complete-run fixture used by c2/c3. Against each, GET /data responds 200 without throwing and GET / renders present fields without crashing. For the corrupt/unparseable state.json served from a cold server start, /data returns 200 with an explicit updating/stale signal — a defined field/value, specifically stale:true (and phase/scores set to their defined fallbacks) — never a 500 or blank error page.
criteria.verifyBy: Fetch-level vitest tests start the server against each of the three degradation fixtures and assert GET /data returns 200 (not 500) with valid JSON and GET / returns 200 HTML. For the missing-fields fixture, assert the exact placeholder token appears where the known-absent field (e.g. budgetSpentUsd) would render. For the mid-run partial, assert present scores render and missing ones are not fabricated. For the corrupt state.json fixture (cold start, no prior good state), assert /data returns 200 with stale:true and the defined fallbacks, and GET / returns 200 without a thrown error or blank page.

criteria.description: npm test passes offline/hermetically with the full new suite green, and the dashboard module imports only Node stdlib (node: builtins) plus local type/read imports — it does not import a harness entrypoint that would pull the Anthropic SDK into the verifier's import closure.
criteria.verifyBy: Run npm test with no network and confirm all prior tests plus the new dashboard tests pass (previously-green baseline preserved); grep the new module's imports to confirm only node: builtins and local type/read imports are present (no heavy harness entrypoint / SDK import).

scope.description: Render only data the run folder already contains. Do not add to or modify the harness's progress-event emission (trace writer, orchestrator, state schema) — Stage 1 is a pure reader over the existing on-disk format.

Strictly read-only: the dashboard must never write, create, or modify any file in the run folder, and must never read or write any config. No launch/stop controls, no config editing (those are stage 2).
No new dependencies — Node built-in modules only; nothing added to package.json dependencies. Tests use the existing vitest devDependency.
True push streaming (SSE/WebSocket) is out of scope; the live feel comes from client-side polling only.
Net-new module + its test file(s) and fixture directories only; do not refactor unrelated harness modules.
